texto: We, the beggar class, have little to lose and our expectations are, at best, modest, and when we suffer, it seems we suffer to the depths, for there is nothing in our lives nor in our souls to buoy our hope. texto: I don’t know how a reporter would ever understand a politician. Your job is supposed to be about finding the truth and enlightening people. Right? texto: As you get older, you have to be willing and able to choose your own path for your life. And once you choose it, you walk it with strength. texto: How do we keep convincing young people to die in fights they didn’t start for reasons we’re too devious to tell the truth about?
